description_long: This is a vintage-style tripod with a brass telescope mounted on top, supported by three wooden legs connected by metal joints and chains for stability. The telescope features a dark metallic body with red accent bands and a textured grip near the eyepiece. The legs are made of polished wood, each attached to the central hub with visible metallic hinges and bolts, and are further stabilized by a chain linking the lower sections. The central hub is robust, constructed from dark metal, and houses the mounting mechanism for the telescope, which appears to be a ball head for flexible adjustment. The overall design is reminiscent of classic surveyor or astronomer equipment, blending both decorative and functional elements. The materials and finish give it an antique, steampunk-inspired appearance suitable for display or practical use in urban or office settings.
description: A vintage brass and wood tripod with a mounted telescope, featuring red accents and metal joints, evoking a classic, steampunk aesthetic.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the brass telescope, red accent bands, and all three wooden legs with metal joints and chains.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly into the telescope lens, highlighting the circular glass and symmetrical arrangement of the tripod legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the telescope's rear, the mounting mechanism, and the tripod's structural details.
description_view_3: Rear view focused on the eyepiece of the telescope and the converging tripod legs, with the chain and bolts clearly visible.
